title: Suddenly CPU All-core 50% from podman service
body: ### Issue Description Image is cookpit dashboard ``/usr/bin/podman --loglevel=info system service`` process occupies 1 thread of CPU usage and reaches 100% usage. ### Steps to reproduce the issue Unknown. ### Describe the results you received I tried `loglevel=debug`, but no logs were generated in dmesg, systemctl, journalctl, etc. when the CPU usage suddenly increased. ``podman ps`` CPU usage per container is less than 1%. ``systemctl --user restart podman.service`` can fix cpu overload. ### Describe the results you expected None. ### podman info output ```yaml flash21@localhost:~$ podman version Client: Podman Engine Version: 5.4.0 API Version: 5.4.0 Go Version: go1.23.9 (Red Hat 1.23.9-1.el10_0) Built: Wed Jun 25 09:00:00 2025 Build Origin: Rocky Linux Build System OS/Arch: linux/amd64 flash21@localhost:~$ podman info host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.39.4 cgroupControllers: - cpu - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.1.12-4.el10.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.12, commit: ' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 86.53 systemPercent: 3.41 userPercent: 10.06 cpus: 20 databaseBackend: sqlite distribution: distribution: rocky version: "10.0" eventLogger: journald freeLocks: 1953 hostname: localhost.localdomain idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1001 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 589824 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1001 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 589824 size: 65536 kernel: 6.12.0-55.27.1.el10_0.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 8831377408 memTotal: 100438831104 networkBackend: netavark networkBackendInfo: backend: netavark dns: package: aardvark-dns-1.14.0-1.el10.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns version: aardvark-dns 1.14.0 package: netavark-1.14.1-1.el10_0.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/netavark version: netavark 1.14.1 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.21-1.el10_0.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.21 commit: 10269840aa07fb7e6b7e1acff6198692d8ff5c88 rundir: /run/user/1001/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux pasta: executable: /usr/bin/pasta package: passt-0^20250217.ga1e48a0-5.el10_0.x86_64 version: "" remoteSocket: exists: true path: /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock rootlessNetworkCmd: pasta security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: true seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: true serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 30095544320 swapTotal: 42949668864 uptime: 209h 40m 23.00s (Approximately 8.71 days) variant: "" plugins: authorization: null log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan - ipvlan volume: - local registries: search: - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.redhat.io - docker.io store: configFile: /home/flash21/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 84 paused: 0 running: 84 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home/flash21/.local/share/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 499861516288 graphRootUsed: 373858193408 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: xfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Supports shifting: "false" Supports volatile: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 119 runRoot: /run/user/1001/containers transientStore: false volumePath: /home/flash21/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 5.4.0 BuildOrigin: Rocky Linux Build System Built: 1750809600 BuiltTime: Wed Jun 25 09:00:00 2025 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.23.9 (Red Hat 1.23.9-1.el10_0) Os: linux OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 5.4.0 flash21@localhost:~$ rpm -q podman podman-5.4.0-12.el10_0.x86_64 flash21@localhost:~$ ``` ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless Rootless ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details None. ### Additional information I have previously run the command below, but I don't think this is the cause. ``truncate -s 0 .local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/*/userdata/ctr.log``

comments.body: Please do `kill -s SIGABRT <pid of service>` when it happens again. That will generate a go stack trace in the service logs and then provide the output here, this should give us some insight what it is doing.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

Here's core dump file (file size is over 25MB ,use external link. uncompression size is 1GB)
used `coredumpctl dump > dimp.txt`(but not txt file)
(link removed)

Note I don't actually want the actual coredump (though it could still be useful I will check)
If a go program gets SIGABRT it will print a go stack stack trace to stderr which is much more read readable. It should be visible in the podman.service logs.

i.e it looks something like this:
```
SIGABRT: abort
PC=0x560201f9f30e m=0 sigcode=0

goroutine 0 gp=0x5602048d4400 m=0 mp=0x5602048d75c0 [idle]:
internal/runtime/syscall.Syscall6()
	/usr/lib/golang/src/internal/runtime/syscall/asm_linux_amd64.s:36 +0xe fp=0x7ffc12d07258 sp=0x7ffc12d07250 pc=0x560201f9f30e
... and much more lines here
```

Yeah I don't think I can get the real coredump file to show anything useful. I tried to load in a rockylinux container with the same rpm version/ and debuginfo symbols but all threads look like this

```
Thread 118 (LWP 1235342):
#0  runtime.usleep () at /usr/lib/golang/src/runtime/sys_linux_amd64.s:135
Backtrace stopped: Cannot access memory at address 0xc0011f1240
```

It might be that I am doing something wrong, I don't really have much dgb experience.
The go stack trace should look better for me to see what the service is doing.

no stdout and spammed containers log from journalctl
May be i need check server monitor, wait for a bit :)

Recently, due to unknown cause, all podman containers were terminated (exit 0) and podman services were killed, so I rebooted my server, so it will take some time to reproduce the cause and collect logs again.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e47ae6741a473ecd76ef92dc20a3d5ef27ac8a30/libpod/events/journal_linux.go#L290-L333

That is basically the relevant code, I don't think there is anything wrong on the podman side at least? We poll only every 5s.
What is triggering the logs read on the client side and are the containers generating a lot of logs? I guess maybe the simple answer is you just have a lot of log activity?

There are many containers, so a lot of logs are output.
First of all, I will reduce the log to a minimum for all containers and update this issue if the problem persists.

It's been about two weeks since I changed podman's log type from the default `journal` to `k8s-file` instead of reducing the logs for individual containers, and the issue has not recurred.
It seems like a bug caused by some issue between the journal log and podman, but since the issue is no longer occurring, I'm closing this issue.
